title: iPhone Features You Should be Using
transcript: [music] For a while now, Apple has been effectively the most important camera company in the world. So, when they announced the new iPhone, I always find it really interesting the way that they talk about the new features. Take the iPhone 17 Pro for example. During the keynote, a lot of people were left wondering who are these features actually for. Apple ProRes RAW, Genlock, Apple Log 2. It all sounds fancy, sounds great, but who actually needs this stuff? And this got me thinking about what I'm going to call the creator pyramid. It's a concept I started to touch on in my review of the 17. And I think it'll give you a much better sense of why Apple chooses some of the features they do, how professionals use those niche features, and then how you can put them to use with your settings, apps, and third party hardware accessories. So, let's take a closer look at this pyramid. I've divided it into four groups. The width of each group kind of represents how many people are producing in that industry. And then vertically, let's say that that represents the complexity of the features that they need to get their job done. Starting at the very top, the smallest group. This is who I'm calling industry professionals. They're working in film, TV, streaming, and commercial production. Creating your favorite shows, movies, and ads. These are generally big teams that can turn into enormous teams depending on the size of the project. I'd say that this group is having the biggest cultural impact per production. And so each movie or show affects the most people around the world and their workflows and tools end up defining a lot of what we think of as professional. Next group are working professionals. And this is the group that I'd put myself and my team into. And there are a lot more of us than the industry pros. We're doing things like event coverage, weddings, product photography, portraits, interviews, training videos, web commercials, client social media, and YouTube channels with higherend productions or more complicated workflows. title: iPhone 17 Pro vs. 16 Pro: ¡No te equivoques! ⚠️
transcript: I spent a year using the iPhone 16 Pro and now that I've used the 17 Pro that came out, I have to tell you that I was wrong.  But I'll explain why.  And after comparing them in all categories, putting them to the test in a real-world user experience, from the simplest to the most extreme, I'm going to tell you which one is really worth buying.  And so that you understand what I'm going to tell you in this video, I first need to give you some context.  When the iPhone 16 Pro came out, I bought it because it's my job to do so , but also because I genuinely love trying new technology, no matter what brand it is .  The problem is that having previously made the switch from the 15 Pro to the 16 Pro, I felt it was exactly the same.  And don't misunderstand me.  Obviously there were better things between one and the other, but they were specific things.  In day-to- day use, it felt exactly like the same phone.  As the year went on, that made me lose interest in him.  So much so that when the date of my 17th birthday approached, I was afraid that something similar would happen. And even more so after having seen many very confusing rumors, because they said that the story was going to be very similar.  But then, what happened?  What is the best option to buy today?  The first thing you need to understand is that we are comparing two very similar phones.  Remember, there's only a year's difference, and Apple isn't the most aggressive when it comes to annual progress.  But still, there are four key factors that will help you decide between going for the newest one or saving that money.  Because yes, even though this is the new one, it also has some serious flaws that you wouldn't have with this one .  Let's start with the design.  The design of these two phones has a much greater impact on your experience with them than just on aesthetics. Ultimately, aesthetically they are quite similar, and although I'm starting to like the aesthetics of the 17 Pro more and more, there are other consequences of that design that make me have my reservations.  Obviously, there are several very positive things. Making the phone thicker has allowed for a larger battery, even larger than that of an S25 Ultra, something never seen before, although we'll talk about the battery in a moment, and for better speakers, and it's true, it sounds louder and better, and although I don't have exact proof that it's largely because it's thicker, I have no doubt either.  But then, what are the problems?  Apple made this phone almost entirely of aluminum so that heat would be conducted better and it wouldn't get so hot.  And if we put a thermal camera on it, we'll see that it is.   That's right .  What's more, something that happened to me when the sun was out was that the 16 Pro would hold up well for a few minutes with good brightness, but after a short while the screen would dim a lot and believe me, it was unbearable to work with it. That doesn't happen to me with the 17 Pro.  I'm not saying it won't happen after a while because it's going to get hot sooner or later anyway, but in my use, which I consider quite intensive when I'm recording the sun and all that, it works wonderfully.  All of this not only results in better performance for longer, but also in the battery not deteriorating as quickly, which is welcome.  But since nothing can be perfect, making an almost entirely aluminum frame also brings some drawbacks.  Just as this metal prevented wireless charging, which is why they had to add this glass part, aluminum and connectivity are not very good friends. In fact, we only need to go back a few years and we'll see all the problems that the iPhone's aluminum designs have caused in that regard.  And although the most logical thing would be to think that Apple has learned from those mistakes, at least for now that has not been the case.  Why am I telling you this?  Since getting the iPhone 17 Pro Max, I, along with thousands of other users, have reported connectivity issues that I didn't have with the 16 Pro. For example, when I leave my house, an area with perfect coverage for any device, the signal drops completely, or when connected to Wi-Fi, there's no internet connection—it just doesn't make sense.  And there are several other problems like this, but the craziest thing is that this happens to me as well as to my wife's 17 Pro and apparently also to several of you who commented on a video I uploaded recently.  Sure, the solution is to turn airplane mode on and off, but please, you can't justify these kinds of problems in a phone at this price point, especially when they dedicated an entire section to talking about how the antennas were around the camera and that this was going to improve everything, and how there's a new chip designed by Apple for connectivity.  Anyway, these things can depend a bit on the specific unit you get, because it does vary, but the percentage is high, and that didn't happen, at least not on a massive scale, with the iPhone 16 Pro. By the way, they were supposed to fix these problems with an update, so Apple knows the issue exists, but I've already updated [music] four times and nothing has changed.  On the other hand, speaking of the screen, they also added an anti-glare coating to the 17 Pro and 17 Pro Max , which isn't the best or anything special compared to the competition, so you're not really going to notice it that much, but it's something to keep in mind.  Moreover, the screen has more nits and it's noticeable in the sun .  Furthermore, as I was saying, this whole design issue also influences performance and battery life.  But before I give you a verdict on that topic, let's talk about the camera, because there are things you should know about it too.  The camera is probably one of the aspects in which these two phones are most similar, but at the same time, the least similar.  On the one hand, they have the same sensor, so both photos and videos are going to be very similar, leaving aside some processing optimizations that Apple makes in the new ones and that it could perfectly bring to the previous ones, but I suppose, what would be the point of it being new?  Furthermore, when it comes to recording videos, both have very similar professional functions, but this is where the difference becomes more noticeable, and it is essential that you understand this as well as possible to know which one suits you best.  You may know that iPhone Pro models from the 15 onwards can record in lock format, which looks flat, but retains more information than a normal video so that when those colors are restored in editing, it has more dynamic range, that is, more detail in the lightest and darkest areas.  But the iPhone 17 Pro has three features that take its camera to the next level, with good news for those who own or prefer the 16 Pro. First , the lock screen I just mentioned for the 15 Pro and 16 Pro is Apple Lock 1, while the 17 Pro uses Apple Lock 2. This new version offers greater color accuracy and more flexibility when editing photos.  Therefore, if you know you're going to edit the color of your videos in post-production, whether you're learning or already a professional in the field, you'll notice a difference, and that's probably why it's in your best interest to go for the new one, especially since it's starting to become an industry standard, even more so than before.  The second factor is that the 17 Pro can also record in RAW format, and this changes absolutely everything.  Because? Well, imagine you're in a scene like this and you didn't have time to change the camera parameters, such as white balance, that is, the temperature, or even the ISO. With the raw format, since it retains as much information as possible, you can make those adjustments after recording.  So if you had any errors in the lighting or the settings, it will be much, much easier to fix.  This is truly a very useful feature, because, to give you an example, when we go to events, things often happen very quickly and we don't always have time to adjust. Everything is perfect, just as I would like it.   By having everything raw, you save yourself the worry of everything going perfectly at the exact moment.  And that's exactly what happens with the third factor too, which is where I have good news.  This third factor is the open gate.  This is basically the ability to record with the entire sensor, and that, instead of giving you a 16-ninths format, gives you much more space to even use the horizontal or vertical part of the same shot.  That's for those who make videos or simply want to .  That's great news, but there's even better news: with the Blackmagic Camera app, this feature is also available for the 16 Pro. Of the three factors I just mentioned, this seems to me to be the most important.  So the fact that it's also in the previous one is a key argument for why the camera shouldn't be the main reason you choose the newer one, unless the other two factors have convinced you.  But what about battery life and performance?  The truth is that every year the iPhone is one of the most powerful phones, but also one with the best battery.  And this is no exception.  But logically the question we want to answer is, is it worth the previous one?  To be direct, yes.  Now I'm going to be clear with you. The 16 Pro, despite not being the best, has an excellent battery that at least for me lasted all day with reasonable use.  The 17 Pro has an average screen-on time of 6 to 7 hours, which is an hour or an hour and a half more than the 16 Pro. This is obviously due to the larger battery , but also to the more efficient processor.  But the A18 Pro, last year's model, is super powerful and there's no task it can't handle either.  So while the numbers obviously favor the 17 Pro, you won't notice virtually any difference in real-world, day-to-day use, and anyone who tells you otherwise is lying.  But then, what is the verdict?  Obviously the new one is new for a reason, but the iPhone 16 Pro or Pro Max, if you can get it at a good price, is still an excellent buy.  It is one of the most solid and stable phones Apple has ever had, in some aspects much more stable than the recently released one.  And what's more, if you find a good deal, the money you save could get you AirPods, an Apple Watch, or simply save you money directly.  Yes, of course, but you will notice differences in certain aspects like battery or camera compared to the new one, and I was wrong to think that this year Apple was going to be just as conservative with the 17. I'm really quite happy with the 17 Pro Max.  I hope this comparison has been helpful. [music]

title: iPhone 17 /Pro/Max: How to turn OFF/ON  (3 Super New Ways)
transcript: Hey, what's up guys? In this video, I will be showcasing you how to switch off the iPhone 17 Pro Max in four different ways. So, let's begin the video. If you wanted to switch off the iPhone 17 series, one method is using by side button and the volume keys. So, press and hold the side button and the volume up or down button together for a few second. You will get this page slide to power off to shut it down. slide this power button to the right and your iPhone will be turned off. Now, in order to switch it back on, hold down the side button for a few second until you see the Apple logo on the screen. Now, release the side button and your iPhone will be turned on. Now, the second method is also really simple. You just need to first go to your settings. Then you will select general and then scroll down till the end and you will get to see the shutdown button. Tap on it and on this page slide this power icon to the right and it will turn off your device. The third method is also really simple. You just need to slide from the top right corner and you will see a power button in here. Just press and hold that power button for a few second and then release it. just slide onto the right side and it will shut down and your iPhone will be turned off. So guys, that is it in this particular video. If this video helped you out, do consider subscribing my channel because some amazing content is planned for this year. Thanks for watching. Peace.

title: Apple iPhone 18 Pro Max vs iPhone 17 Pro Max — Should You Wait? 8 BIG Reasons to Hold Off
transcript: It's been just a few weeks since Apple officially released the iPhone 17 series. And as always, the question that dominates discussions online and my comment section is a simple yet crucial one. Should I buy an iPhone 17 Pro or Pro Max now or wait for the iPhone 18 Pro or Pro Max? This is not just a casual question. It's a decision that could affect your daily tech experience for the next couple of years. On one hand, the iPhone 17 Pro Max is a high-erformance device packed with impressive upgrades over older iPhones. On the other, rumors and leaks surrounding the upcoming iPhone 18 Pro Max suggest that Apple could be introducing significant game-changing innovations that may make waiting extremely worthwhile. If you're coming from an older device, say an iPhone 11 or 12 series, the upgrade to an iPhone 17 Pro Max still makes sense. You're getting a far superior processor, updated cameras, and modern design refinements that will feel like a generational leap. But if your current device is newer, like an iPhone 15 Pro Max or iPhone 16 Pro Max, the decision becomes trickier. According to reliable leaks and industry insiders, the iPhone 18 Pro Max could deliver advances in performance, display technology, photography, battery life, and overall user experience that make the jump more enticing than the 17 series. Today, we're going to break down eight compelling reasons why waiting for the iPhone 18 Pro Max might be the smarter choice, along with a detailed explanation of what's coming in 2026. Whether you're a casual user or a power user who demands peak performance, this guide will help you make an informed decision. One, the dynamic island evolution. Cleaner, sleeker, smarter. Let's start with design. More specifically, the front display. One of the most talked about features in Apple's recent lineup has been the dynamic island. Introduced with the iPhone 14 Pro, it replaced the classic notch with a small pillshaped cutout for the front-facing camera and Face ID sensors. Since then, the dynamic island has remained largely unchanged from the 14 Pro Max all the way to the iPhone 17 Pro Max. The iPhone 18 Pro Max, however, might change everything. Sources suggest that Apple could dramatically shrink the dynamic island, potentially reducing its size by 50% or more. Imagine reclaiming that extra screen real estate for apps, video, and gaming. your full screen experience would feel less obstructed and more immersive. Some rumors even hint at Face ID being integrated beneath the display, leaving only a tiny hole punch for the selfie camera. While both approaches are speculative, most reports lean toward a substantially smaller island as the likely outcome. This is thanks to advancements in Face ID sensor miniaturization, which have been steadily improving since their debut with the iPhone 10. Even though the front-facing camera may remain the same 18 megapixel sensor introduced with the 17 series, housing it in a smaller dynamic island creates a sleeker, more modern appearance. For many users, this alone is reason enough to wait for the 18 Pro Max rather than upgrading to the 17 Pro Max immediately. Two, performance like never before. The A22 Nanm chip. Apple has a well-earned reputation for pushing performance leaps every generation, and the iPhone 18 series is rumored to be no exception. The A20 Pro chip built on tunometer technology is shaping up to be one of Apple's most powerful processors ever. Currently, the A19 chip in the iPhone 17 is manufactured on a 3 nanometer process, which is already highly efficient. However, moving to tuninometer fabrication allows Apple to pack billions more transistors into the same chip space. The result, dramatically improved CPU and GPU performance, better energy efficiency, and enhanced support for AI and machine learning tasks. Industry analysts predict that the performance jump from the A19 to the A20 could feel much more significant than the incremental upgrades between previous generations. Tasks like multitasking, gaming, video editing, and real-time AI processing will run smoother than ever before. Apple has also reportedly designed the iPhone 18 series to efficiently manage heat. The vapor chamber cooling system first introduced in the iPhone 17 Pro Max continues to evolve, ensuring the A20 chip stays cool even under heavy workloads. Paired with 12 GB of faster RAM, the iPhone 18 Pro Max could offer lightning fast multitasking and seamless app performance, setting a new B. Benchmark for smartphone computing power. Three, revolutionary camera system, variable aperture, and Samsung sensor. Photography enthusiasts are in for a treat. While Apple has steadily improved the camera system year over year, the iPhone 18 Pro Max is rumored to include a revolutionary main sensor featuring variable aperture technology. Unlike a fixed aperture, which is currently standard, a variable aperture lens can adjust automatically to lighting conditions, allowing for more natural and balanced photos in both bright and low light environments. Think of it as bringing a DSLR level camera experience to a smartphone where manual aperture control could be available in pro mode for photographers who want complete control over their shots. Another exciting rumor, Apple is said to be switching back to Samsung sensors for the main wide camera, replacing Sony sensors used in previous iPhones. Samsung's sensor technology could provide better dynamic range, improved low light performance, and more precise color reproduction. While the ultra wide and telephoto lenses may seem minor or no changes, the main sensor upgrade alone could redefine smartphone photography. Whether you're shooting casual photos or professional quality content, the iPhone 18 Pro Max could offer capabilities that surpass the iPhone 17 Pro Max by a wide margin. Four, battery life and charging. Carbon based cells. Battery technology is another area where the iPhone 18 series could leap ahead. The iPhone 17 Pro Max already offers solid endurance, but Apple may be adopting carbon-based battery cells in the iPhone 18 Pro Max. These advanced batteries promise faster charging, longer lifespan, and higher energy density compared to traditional lithium-ion cells. Combined with the energyefficient A20 chip and other internal optimizations, users could experience longer battery life even with heavy use, including gaming, streaming, and AR applications. Five, connectivity improvements. The C2 communication chip. Connectivity is evolving alongside hardware. Rumors indicate that Apple will introduce a new C2 or C2X communication chip in the iPhone 18 series. This chip is expected to handle 5G, Wi-Fi 7, and Bluetooth more efficiently than current solutions. The impact of this upgrade, faster network speeds, reduced latency, and better battery conservation. Enhanced wireless performance will improve everything from streaming 4K per 8K content to cloud gaming and AR/VR applications, making the iPhone 18 Pro Max not just a smartphone, but nextG connectivity powerhouse. Six aesthetic refinements, ceramic glass, and subtle transparency. While Apple is not expected to dramatically overhaul the overall design for 2026, there are subtle yet exciting tweaks. One rumored upgrade is ceramic glass over the MagSafe coil on the back, possibly with a slight translucency. This would allow users to see hints of the internal components like the MagSafe coil or battery without using a transparent case. It's a small aesthetic upgrade, but for those who care about the visual sophistication of their device, it could be a compelling reason to wait. Seven, release timing and pricing. For those wondering when this device will be available, all signs point to September 2026 as the official unveiling of the iPhone 18 lineup. Pricing is expected to remain in line with the iPhone 17 series, meaning roughly $1,049 for the Pro and $1,249 for the Pro Max in the US. This makes the decision even more interesting. For the same investment, users might gain substantially improved performance, photography, battery life, and display technology. Eight, future proofing your investment. Finally, one of the most compelling reasons to wait is futurep proofing. With Apple's rumored leap in chip technology, camera capabilities, and connectivity, the iPhone 18 Pro Max could remain top tier for longer, supporting new iOS features, AIdriven apps, and augmented reality experiences for years to come. Upgrading to the iPhone 17 Pro Max now may feel great, but if you want to maximize longevity, speed, and innovation, waiting for the iPhone 18 Pro Max could be the smarter move. Final thoughts. In summary, here's why waiting for the iPhone 18 Pro Max could be advantageous. Dynamic Island gets smaller and cleaner, more screen space for you. A 22 nanometer chip offers huge speed and efficiency gains. Camera revolution with variable aperture and Samsung sensor. Carbon-based battery extends endurance and reduces charging time. C2 connectivity chip for faster Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, and 5G efficiency. Subtle design tweaks like translucent ceramic glass over Mag Safe. September 2026 release aligns with pricing similar to iPhone 17. Futureproof technology ensures the device stays relevant longer. If you're currently on an older iPhone, iPhone 11 or earlier, upgrading to the iPhone 17 Pro Max still makes sense. But if you're on a recent model, the iPhone 18 Pro Max could be worth the wait, delivering next level performance, photography, and efficiency.
